title: Periscope Reflection Lab
wsObjectives: Use models to explain scientific ideas
Identify variables in an optical setup

effectTimelines.label: Ray trace
effectTimelines.description: A torch beam travels into the periscope, reflects from the mirrors and either reaches the eyepiece or misses it.

steps.title: Define the optics question
steps.prompt: How does mirror angle affect whether light reaches the viewer through a periscope?
steps.guidance: Choose the question that best matches the pattern you want to explore.

options.label: How does mirror angle affect the ray path?
How does mirror angle affect target visibility?

steps.title: Plan the model investigation
steps.prompt: Choose the variable you will change and the conditions you will keep steady.
steps.guidance: The periscope shape and the light source must stay the same for a fair model test.

options.label: Keep the target position the same
Keep the periscope shape the same
Keep the torch direction the same

steps.title: Predict the reflection path
steps.prompt: Predict which mirror angle will guide the light beam down the periscope to the viewer.
steps.guidance: Study the setup preview first, then think about how reflection changes the beam direction.
steps.sentenceStem: I predict ___ will work best because ___.

steps.title: Trace the periscope beam
steps.prompt: Choose a mirror angle and target, then trace the beam from the torch through the periscope.
steps.guidance: Watch whether the beam reaches the eyepiece or hits the inside of the model.

steps.title: Record what happened
steps.prompt: Record which mirror angle let the target be seen and describe what happened to the beam.
steps.guidance: Use the traced light path as evidence.

steps.title: Analyse the pattern
steps.prompt: What pattern do you notice between mirror angle and whether the target could be seen?
steps.guidance: Compare at least two traced paths.

steps.title: Conclude using evidence
steps.prompt: Explain how reflection helped the beam reach the viewer in the most successful trial.
steps.guidance: Refer to the mirror angle and the ray path.

steps.title: Evaluate the model
steps.prompt: What part of this light model is simplified compared with a real periscope?
steps.guidance: Think about perfectly visible rays, perfect mirrors and fixed light paths.

teacherNotes: The beam is visible on purpose so pupils can talk through the reflection path.
Use the eyepiece outcome to connect beam direction with whether the target can be seen.
safetyNote: Real periscope builds may involve mirrors and cutting materials; use teacher-approved equipment and safe handling routines.
misconceptionChecks: The visible beam is a model to show direction, not literal visible light in every real situation.
The mirrors redirect the light; they do not create the light.

hero.blurb: Trace a torch beam through a procedural periscope and test which mirror angles actually let the viewer see the target.
